---
title: "Roadmap"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Roadmap}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

```{r render-roadmap, results = "asis"}
## Resolve the path to ROADMAP.md robustly across render contexts.
##
## Why this is not a `child = "../../ROADMAP.md"` chunk:
## `pkgdown::build_site()` runs each article through `callr::r_safe()` which
## copies the .Rmd into a tempdir before rendering; a relative `child =
## "../../ROADMAP.md"` chunk path then resolves to `/ROADMAP.md` (the system
## root) and fails with "Permission denied". This bit us once already
## (pkgdown workflow run #94 on commit `d345ca1`). Local
## `pkgdown::build_article("articles/roadmap")` does NOT use the tmp-copy
## path, so the relative `child =` works locally -- but CI does not.
##
## The fix: read `ROADMAP.md` programmatically (no knitr `child = ` file
## copy) and try several plausible locations. The package source root is
## what we want; we try a few angles.

candidate_paths <- c(
  "ROADMAP.md",         # pkgdown CI: knit_root_dir = pkg root
  "../../ROADMAP.md"    # local rmarkdown::render from vignettes/articles/
)

## rprojroot fallback: walks up the directory tree looking for DESCRIPTION;
## errors if the tree doesn't contain a package root. Wrap in tryCatch.
rproj_path <- tryCatch(
  file.path(rprojroot::find_package_root_file(), "ROADMAP.md"),
  error = function(e) NA_character_
)
if (!is.na(rproj_path)) {
  candidate_paths <- c(candidate_paths, rproj_path)
}

existing <- Filter(file.exists, candidate_paths)
if (length(existing) == 0L) {
  stop("ROADMAP.md not found in any of: ",
       paste(candidate_paths, collapse = ", "))
}
roadmap_path <- existing[[1L]]

cat(readLines(roadmap_path, encoding = "UTF-8"), sep = "\n")
```
